articles.title: China gives suspended death sentences to 2 ex-defense ministers
articles.summary: Both were the latest to be sentenced in President Xi Jinping's ongoing anti-corruption campaign, which started more than a decade ago.

articles.title: Trump Panel Recommends FEMA Respond to Fewer Disasters
articles.summary: A White House task force called for speeding up aid and responding to fewer disasters. But some of its ideas would require action by Congress to become reality.

articles.title: Trump Hosts Brazil’s President Lula at White House After Months of Ups and Downs
articles.summary: President Trump and President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva, who have had a rocky relationship, met for talks on trade and other issues. But they skipped a planned joint appearance.

articles.title: She helps families in need. As gas and grocery prices rise, she needs help, too
articles.summary: She has spent decades helping others struggling to make ends meet. Now the rising cost of gas and groceries has left Dalene Basden feeling the strain herself.

articles.title: Confused about the abortion pill news? Here's where things stand
articles.summary: The courts are considering ending telemedicine access to mifepristone, one of the two pills used in a medication abortion. Here's what's happened this week and what's to come.

articles.title: As Stockpiles Fall, U.S. Sells More Missiles Worth $17 Billion to Gulf Nations
articles.summary: The United States and its partners have burned through an enormous amount of air defense missiles in the Iran war, and production is slow.

articles.title: Former U.S. analyst, once jailed for spying for Israel, to run for Israeli parliament
articles.summary: Jonathan Pollard, a former U.S. intelligence analyst who served 30 years for spying for Israel, says he will run in the upcoming Israeli elections.

articles.title: A Trump council recommends overhauling FEMA. Here are 3 key changes
articles.summary: A group of emergency experts wants the Trump administration to raise the bar for federal help after disasters, and also make it easier for survivors to get money quickly.

articles.title: Health Officials Race to Track Hantavirus Cruise Ship Outbreak but Predict ‘Limited’ Spread
articles.summary: A Dutch flight attendant and French national were among the latest to be tested in connection with a deadly outbreak on a cruise ship. But an analysis in South Africa showed no mutations in viral samp

articles.title: Appeals Court Looks Unlikely to Allow Hegseth to Punish Mark Kelly for Video
articles.summary: A three-judge panel in Washington heard arguments in the lawsuit aimed at stopping the Pentagon from disciplining Senator Mark Kelly for a video warning about illegal military orders.

articles.title: DHS blames funding lapse for shutdown of internal detention oversight
articles.summary: The office investigates detainee deaths and access to medical care, among other issues. It's being wound down, even as the number of detention deaths and length of detention stays has grown.

articles.title: A judge unsealed Epstein's purported 2019 suicide note. More documents could follow
articles.summary: At the request of the New York Times, a judge unsealed a goodbye note that Jeffrey Epstein's former cellmate says he found after the convicted sex offender's first suicide attempt in July 2019.

articles.title: Tennessee Republicans pass a map to break up the state's lone Democratic House seat
articles.summary: Tennessee Republicans' map would crack Shelby County — home to majority-Black Memphis — into three different districts, in an effort to eliminate the state's lone remaining Democratic-held seat.

articles.title: House Republican Proposes Bill to Wind Down the Iran War
articles.summary: Representative Tom Barrett, a Michigan Republican facing a tough re-election race, introduced a bill to impose limits on the use of military force in Iran and end the fighting this summer.

articles.title: U.S. Debt Hits a Concerning Milestone, and Experts Say Trump’s Policies Could Worsen It
articles.summary: The debt is outgrowing the size of America’s economy. The president’s policies could accelerate the country’s fiscal headaches, experts say, unless policymakers intervene.

articles.title: Why Stocks and Bonds Are Responding Differently to the Iran War
articles.summary: Stock investors are betting that companies will make enormous profits, despite the war. But investors in bonds, including U.S. Treasuries, have other concerns.

articles.title: Rubio visits Vatican amid escalating tensions between Trump and Pope Leo
articles.summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio traveled to Rome in an effort to preserve a crucial relationship between Washington and the Holy See.

articles.title: Iran reviews U.S. proposal. And, Rubio to meet Pope Leo after Trump's criticism
articles.summary: Iran is reviewing the Trump administration's latest proposal to end the war. And, Secretary of State Marco Rubio is meeting Pope Leo XIV amid President Trump's harsh criticism of him.

articles.title: Dirty nickel: The cost of mining in Indonesia
articles.summary: Across six locations in Indonesia, NPR spoke with locals about how nickel mining is changing the land and daily life. It's brought jobs, but also concerns about environmental damage and public health.

articles.title: Health officials track dozens who left hantavirus-stricken ship after 1st fatality
articles.summary: Oceanwide Expeditions has revealed that 30 passengers disembarked from its cruise ship hit by a hantavirus outbreak on April 24, on the remote island of St. Helena, nearly two weeks after the first pa

articles.title: Affected by the Iran war, residents in Southeast Asian countries voice their concerns
articles.summary: Southeast Asian countries are voicing concern about President Trump and the war with Iran.

articles.title: Secretary of State Marco Rubio meets with Pope Leo after Trump's criticism
articles.summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio meets with Pope Leo after critical comments by President Trump.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
